metadata.summary: The Bootstrap aggregating version of the great SCM

documentation:
## randomSCM

The Bootstrap aggregating version of the great SCM

## Installation

from PyPI :

``` 
pip install randomscm
```

from source code :

```
git clone git@github.com:thibgo/randomscm.git
python -m pip install .
```

## Usage
```
from randomscm import RandomScmClassifier

classifier = RandomScmClassifier()
```

Classifier implements most of scikit-learn standard methods.


## Contributors
 * [Thibaud Godon](https://github.com/thibgo) (maintainer)
 * [Elina Francovic-Fontaine](https://github.com/ElinaFF)
 * [Baptiste Bauvin](https://github.com/babau1)

documentation: Fit SCM estimators on subsamples of the training data

functions.name: randomscm.randomscm._parallel_predict_proba
documentation: Compute predictions of SCM estimators

functions.name: randomscm.randomscm._partition_estimators
documentation: Private function used to partition estimators between jobs.

classes.name: randomscm.randomscm.FakeEstim
documentation:
Fake estimator used as a decoy if there is only one class in a specific sub-sampling.
Does nothing, and has no impact on feature importance.

classes.name: randomscm.randomscm.RandomScmClassifier

methods.name: randomscm.randomscm.RandomScmClassifier.p_for_estimators
documentation: Return the value of p for each estimator to fit.

methods.name: randomscm.randomscm.RandomScmClassifier.get_estimators
documentation: Return the list of estimators of the classifier

methods.name: randomscm.randomscm.RandomScmClassifier.get_hyperparams
documentation: Return the model hyperparameters

methods.name: randomscm.randomscm.RandomScmClassifier.labels_conversion
documentation: Return the equivalence between labels and binaries

methods.name: randomscm.randomscm.RandomScmClassifier.fit
documentation: Fit the model with the given data

methods.name: randomscm.randomscm.RandomScmClassifier.predict
documentation:
Compute model predictions for data in X

Returns:
----------
predictions : array
    predictions[i] is the predicted class for he sample i in X

methods.name: randomscm.randomscm.RandomScmClassifier.predict_proba
documentation:
Predict class probabilities according to the model estimators

Parameters :
----------
X : array
    a dataset to predict

Returns : 
----------
proba : array
    proba[c] contains the estimated probability for each sample of X to belong to class c

methods.name: randomscm.randomscm.RandomScmClassifier.features_importance
documentation:
Returns an array with the importance value of each feature
Importance is computed as the ponderated number of use of the feature in the rules

methods.name: randomscm.randomscm.RandomScmClassifier.all_data_tiebreaker
documentation:
Choose a rule between rule with equal utility
Select the one that have the best accuracy if applied alone on all the data

Parameters :
----------
model_type : strint ('conjunction' or 'dijunction')
    type of the model
feature_idx, thresholds, rule_type : arrays
    description of the rules
X, y : arrays
    a dataset used to compare rules

Returns:
----------
ID of the rule to select

methods.name: randomscm.randomscm.RandomScmClassifier.get_estimators_indices
documentation: Get drawn indices along both sample and feature axes

A Bagging classifier for SetCoveringMachineClassifier()
The base estimators are built on subsets of both samples
and features.
Parameters
\----------
n_estimators : int, default=10

> The number of base estimators in the ensemble.

max_samples

: The number of samples to draw from X to train each base estimator without
  replacement.
  Can be 'sqrt', 'log2', 'auto', None, int or float
  \- If int, then draw 'max_samples' samples.
  \- If float, then draw 'max_samples * X.shape[0]' samples.

max_features

: The number of features to draw from X to train each base estimator (
  without replacement.
  Can be 'sqrt', 'log2', 'auto', None, int or float
  \- If int, then draw 'max_features' features.
  \- If float, then draw 'max_features * X.shape[1]' features.

max_rules

: maximal number of rules for the scm estimators

p

: The estimators will be fitted with the value of p or the values of p found in the list
  If p is a list,

  > let k be k = n_estimators/len(list),
  > the k first estimators will have p=list[0],
  > the next k estimators will have p=list[1] and so on...

model_type

: type of estimators to build
  accepted values : 'conjunction', 'disjunction'

n_jobs

: The number of jobs to run in parallel for both fit and
  predict. 'None' means 1. '-1' means using all
  processors.

random_state

: Controls the random resampling of the original dataset
  (sample wise and feature wise).
  If the base estimator accepts a `random_state` attribute, a different
  seed is generated for each instance in the ensemble.
  Pass an int for reproducible output across multiple function calls.
  See {term}`Glossary <random_state>`.

# Attributes

[n_features]

: The number of features when {meth}`fit` is performed.

[estimators]

: The collection of fitted base estimators.

estim_features

: The subset of drawn features for each base estimator.

# References

[^footnote-1]: L. Breiman, "Pasting small votes for classification in large
    databases and on-line", Machine Learning, 36(1), 85-103, 1999.

[^footnote-2]: G. Louppe and P. Geurts, "Ensembles on Random Patches", Machine
    Learning and Knowledge Discovery in Databases, 346-361, 2012.
